info.dc:title: GitHub Repository Graphs as Auditable Feed-Forward Networks for Coding Agent Reasoning
info.dc:creator: metavacua (Claude Sonnet 4.6, 2026-06-29)
info.dc:subject: language models; feed-forward networks; knowledge graphs; auditable AI; coding agents; larql; WalkFfn; gate-KNN; vindex; retrieval-augmented generation
info.dc:description: We present an architecture in which a GitHub repository's call graph, dependency graph, and environment-seam graph are encoded as a vector-indexed triple store and used as the feed-forward network (FFN) layer of a language model inference session. Unlike opaque neural FFN weights, this externalised representation exposes every retrieval step — entity embedding, gate-vector similarity score, feature slot, and target token — making the coding agent's factual associations fully auditable post-hoc. We describe the implementation in terms of the larql WalkFfn gate-KNN mechanism, report a critical batch-insert collision bug in the larql-python bindings (VectorIndex.find_free_feature mmap/heap split), and propose a dual-theory experimental design (topological vs. spectral layer assignment) whose observable divergence settles whether WalkFfn encodes weighted BFS structure or global spectral structure.
